nodes.title: Derive total pages from data size
nodes.why: The page count follows the current collection and still provides one stable page for an empty dataset.
nodes.alternative: Hard-code the number of pages independently from the rows.
nodes.tradeoff: A minimum of one simplifies controls, though some products may prefer an explicit zero-page empty state.

nodes.title: Derive only the visible rows
nodes.why: A non-mutating slice keeps the source data intact while exposing exactly one page.
nodes.alternative: Splice the canonical users array as navigation changes.
nodes.tradeoff: The getter creates a small array per evaluation, but navigation remains reversible and predictable.

nodes.title: Stop navigation at the last page
nodes.why: The guard keeps currentPage inside the derived page range even if the method is called directly.
nodes.alternative: Rely only on a disabled Next button in the template.
nodes.tradeoff: Duplicating the boundary in component logic makes the method robust for keyboard and programmatic callers.
